1.description: SFX-implied and observed depreciation rates. We plot the depreciation rates implied by the SFX model (blue, vertical axis left) against the observed depreciation rates (red, vertical axis right). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Maximal Sharpe ratios. We decompose conditional volatility of the pricing kernel into the components associated with bond innovations ε,λt⊤λt$\sqrt {\lambda _t^\top \lambda _t}$(blue line, Bond MSR), and depreciation rate innovations η that are unspanned by bonds,γt⊤γt$\sqrt {\gamma _t^\top \gamma _t}$(red line, FX MSR). The corresponding 95% confidence bands are depicted in lighter colors. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Term structure of carry returns. We plot the unconditional average annual return of a cross-sectional carry trade as a function of the maturity of the bonds used for borrowing and lending. The trading strategy uses the slope of the yield curve (120-month yield minus 12-month yield) as the sorting variable to create cross-sectional dispersion. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: News-based decomposition of currency risk premiums, British pound. We plot the percentage contribution to news about British pound risk premiums, according to1=var(Ns,tn)/var(Nsrp,tn)+var(NΔy,tn)/var(Nsrp,tn)−2cov(Ns,tn,NΔy,tn)/var(Nsrp,tn)$1= \text{var}(N^n_{s,t})/ \text{var}(N^n_{srp,t}) + \text{var}(N^n_{\Delta y,t})/ \text{var}(N^n_{srp,t}) - 2 \text{cov}(N^n_{s,t}, N^n_{\Delta y,t})/ \text{var}(N^n_{srp,t})$, across the different horizonsn. We use three models to compute the decomposition: UFX, SFX, and real and nominal versions of the Bansal-Shaliastovich model. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
